zh.lifeSummary: 當John de Mowbray於1310年11月29日出生在英國越可爾霍文加姆時，他的父親John de Mowbray 2nd Baron Mowbray24歲，母親Baroness Aline de Braose24歲。他於1322年2月26日與Maud de Holand結婚。他去世於1361年10月4日在英國英格蘭越可爾約克享年50歲，埋葬在英國波克夏雷亞丁格也福利亞斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John de Mowbray于1310年11月29日出生在英国越可爾霍文加姆时，他的父亲John de Mowbray 2nd Baron Mowbray24岁，母亲Baroness Aline de Braose24岁。他于1322年2月26日与Maud de Holand结婚。他去世于1361年10月4日在英国英格兰越可爾約克享年50岁，埋葬在英国波克夏雷亞丁格也福利亞斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Mowbray
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern; of Norman origin): habitational name from Montbray (Manche). Robert de Mowbray was Earl of Northumbria 1086–95, but was later imprisoned and deposed for rebelling against William II.

variants.name: Mulberry
meanings: English (Middlesex and Cambridgeshire): variant of   Mowbray  .
Irish (Derry): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Maoilbhearaigh ‘descendant of the devotee of (Saint) Bearach’.

variants.name: Mayberry
meanings: English: most probably an altered form of   Mowbray  . It is also found as   Maybury  , which has the form of an English habitational name. There is a place near Woking in Surrey so called; however, this is not recorded until 1885 and is probably derived from the surname. In England this surname is found mainly in the West Midlands; it has also spread into Wales. In Ireland this form is common in Ulster; MacLysaght records that it was taken there from England in the 17th century.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When John de Mowbray was born on 29 November 1310, in Hovingham, Yorkshire, England, his father, John de Mowbray 2nd Baron Mowbray, was 24 and his mother, Baroness Aline de Braose, was 24. He married Maud de Holand on 26 February 1322. He died on 4 October 1361, in York, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 50, and was buried in Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John de Mowbray est né le 29 novembre 1310, à Hovingham, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, John de Mowbray 2nd Baron Mowbray, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Baroness Aline de Braose, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Maud de Holand le 26 février 1322. Il est décédé le 4 octobre 1361, à York, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterré à Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als John de Mowbray am 29. November 1310 in Hovingham, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de Mowbray 2nd Baron Mowbray 24 und seine Mutter Baroness Aline de Braose 24. Er hat am 26. Februar 1322 Maud de Holand geheiratet. Er starb am 4. Oktober 1361 im Alter von 50 Jahren in York, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John de Mowbray è nato il il 29 novembre 1310 nel Hovingham, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, John de Mowbray 2nd Baron Mowbray, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Baroness Aline de Braose, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Maud de Holand il 26 febbraio 1322. Morì il 4 ottobre 1361, nel York, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 50 anni, e fu sepolto a Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: John de Mowbrayが1310年11月29日にイギリスヨークシュアイアホーヴィンガムで生まれた時，父John de Mowbray 2nd Baron Mowbrayは24歳，母Baroness Aline de Braoseは24歳であった。1322年2月26日にMaud de Holandと結婚した。彼は1361年10月4日にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアヨークで50歳で死去し，イギリスバークシュアイアレアディンググレイフリアルズ チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John de Mowbray(이)가 1310년 11월 29일 영국 요르크실르 호빙함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Mowbray 2nd Baron Mowbray(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Baroness Aline de Braose(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1322년 2월 26일 Maud de Holand(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1361년 10월 4일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 요르크에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 버크실르 리딩 그리프리아르스 처르치에 묻혔다.

pt.29 November 1310–4 October 1361: 29 de novembro de 1310–4 de outubro de 1361
pt.lifeSummary: Quando John de Mowbray nasceu em 29 de novembro de 1310, em Hovingham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de Mowbray 2nd Baron Mowbray, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Baroness Aline de Braose, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud de Holand em 26 de fevereiro de 1322. Ele faleceu em 4 de outubro de 1361, em York, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 50 anos, e foi sepultado em Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John de Mowbray родился 29 ноября 1310 г. в местности ховингхам, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени John de Mowbray 2nd Baron Mowbray было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Baroness Aline de Braose, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud de Holand 26 февраля 1322 г. Он умер 4 октября 1361 г. в местности ерк, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 50 лет и был похоронен в местности грифрярс чурч, ридинг, биркшир, Англия.

es.29 November 1310–4 October 1361: 29 de noviembre de 1310–4 de octubre de 1361
es.lifeSummary: Cuando John de Mowbray nació el 29 de noviembre de 1310, en Hovingham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John de Mowbray 2nd Baron Mowbray, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Baroness Aline de Braose, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Maud de Holand el 26 de febrero de 1322. Murió el 4 de octubre de 1361, en York, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 50 años, y fue sepultado en Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, Inglaterra.
